title: Forms Available to File Online | USCIS
content:
## Forms Available to File Online
Alert: We now offer online filing of Form I-485, Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status , for certain employment-based applicants and their representatives. We are in the process of developing the online filing capability for other applicant types.
When you file Form I-485, you must also submit Form I-693, Report of Immigration Medical Examination and Vaccination Record . A civil surgeon is responsible for providing you with a completed Form I-693 that is signed and placed in a sealed envelope. If you file Form I-485 online, you must open the sealed envelope from the civil surgeon and upload the completed Form I-693 with your application package. You must keep the original Form I-693 and the envelope from the civil surgeon until we make a final decision on your Form I-485. We may review the original Form I-693 and envelope during an interview or ask you to submit the original Form I-693 and envelope as evidence to support your application.
If you file Form I-485 by mail, there are no changes to the Form I-693 submission process. You must submit Form I-693 with your Form I-485 application package, in the original sealed envelope provided by the civil surgeon.
This page lists the USCIS forms you can file online. Visit the File Online page to learn about the benefits of filing a form using our secure online system. Start by creating a free USCIS online account .
If you are immigrating to the United States as a lawful permanent resident, you must pay the USCIS Immigrant Fee online, unless you are exempted from the fee.
If you are filing Form I-131A, Application for Carrier Documentation, you must pay the filing fee for this form online.
There are 2 options for submitting forms using a USCIS online account:
- Fill out a form through a guided online workflow; or
- Upload a completed PDF of the form.
The available submission options depend on the form type and filing category. Both submission options give you access to the same USCIS online account case management features.
There is a discount when filing certain forms online. You can find specific form filing fee information on our Fee Schedule page .

#### Fill Out Form Online
This option allows you to complete the form electronically through a step-by-step process where you only answer questions relevant to you. Below is a list of forms you can file using this option.
AR-11 | Alien’s Change of Address Card Please visit our How To Change Your Address page for additional information.
AR-11 | Alien’s Change of Address Card
- Please visit our How To Change Your Address page for additional information.
G-325R | Biographic Information (Registration)
G-639 | Freedom of Information/Privacy Act and Online FOIA Request
I-90 | Application to Replace Permanent Resident Card
I-129 | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker USCIS will accept Form I-129 for non-cap H-1B petitions. USCIS will accept Form I-129 for H-1B cap petitions for petitioners whose registrations have been selected.
I-129 | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
- USCIS will accept Form I-129 for non-cap H-1B petitions.
- USCIS will accept Form I-129 for H-1B cap petitions for petitioners whose registrations have been selected.
I-130 | Petition for Alien Relative
I-131 | Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records Form I-131 Application Type Selection on Form I-131 Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States but are not applying under a specific parole program or process. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else Part 1, Box 7 on Form I-131 Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process: Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021; Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022; The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program; The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative; The Central American Minors Program; or Certain participants in the FRTF Process Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021) Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022) Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program) Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative) Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program) Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement) You cannot change or amend your selected application type after you file online. If you file Form I-131 online for a category that must be filed on paper (for example, to request an advance parole document) but indicate another category to enable online filing, we may deny your Form I-131 and we will not refund your Form I-131 filing fee.
I-131 | Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records
Form I-131 Application Type
Selection on Form I-131
Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States but are not applying under a specific parole program or process. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else
Part 1, Box 7 on Form I-131
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process: Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021; Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022; The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program; The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative; The Central American Minors Program; or Certain participants in the FRTF Process
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process:
- Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021;
- Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022;
- The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program;
- The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative;
- The Central American Minors Program; or
- Certain participants in the FRTF Process
Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021) Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022) Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program) Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative) Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program) Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement)
- Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021)
- Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022)
- Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program)
- Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative)
- Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program)
- Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement)
You cannot change or amend your selected application type after you file online. If you file Form I-131 online for a category that must be filed on paper (for example, to request an advance parole document) but indicate another category to enable online filing, we may deny your Form I-131 and we will not refund your Form I-131 filing fee.
I-539 | Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status USCIS is accepting Form I-907 requests, filed via paper form or online, for applicants seeking a change of status to F-1, F-2, M-1, M-2, J-1, or J-2 status, who have a pending Form I-539, Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status, or when filed together with Form I-539.
I-539 | Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
- USCIS is accepting Form I-907 requests, filed via paper form or online, for applicants seeking a change of status to F-1, F-2, M-1, M-2, J-1, or J-2 status, who have a pending Form I-539, Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status, or when filed together with Form I-539.
I-589 | Application for Asylum and Withholding of Removal
I-765 | Application for Employment Authorization (a)(12) – Temporary Protected Status granted (c)(3)(A) – F-1 student, pre-completion Optional Practical Training (OPT); (c)(3)(B) – F-1 student, post-completion OPT; (c)(3)(C) – F-1 student, 24-month extension of OPT for science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) students; (c)(8) – Application for Employment Authorization Based on Pending Form I-589, Application for Asylum or for Withholding of Removal, and Applicants for Pending Asylum under the ABC Settlement Agreement; (c)(11) – Paroled for urgent humanitarian or significant public benefit purposes; (c)(19) – Certain pending TPS applicants who USCIS has determined are prima facie eligible for TPS and who may then receive an EAD as a “temporary treatment benefit” under 8 C.F.R. 244.10(a); or (c)(33) – Consideration of Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) You must have a pending or approved Form I-821D to file Form I-765.
I-765 | Application for Employment Authorization
- (a)(12) – Temporary Protected Status granted
- (c)(3)(A) – F-1 student, pre-completion Optional Practical Training (OPT);
- (c)(3)(B) – F-1 student, post-completion OPT;
- (c)(3)(C) – F-1 student, 24-month extension of OPT for science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) students;
- (c)(8) – Application for Employment Authorization Based on Pending Form I-589, Application for Asylum or for Withholding of Removal, and Applicants for Pending Asylum under the ABC Settlement Agreement;
- (c)(11) – Paroled for urgent humanitarian or significant public benefit purposes;
- (c)(19) – Certain pending TPS applicants who USCIS has determined are prima facie eligible for TPS and who may then receive an EAD as a “temporary treatment benefit” under 8 C.F.R. 244.10(a); or
- (c)(33) – Consideration of Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) You must have a pending or approved Form I-821D to file Form I-765.
I-821 | Application for Temporary Protected Status
I-821D | Consideration of Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals
I-907 | Request for Premium Processing Service Filed either via paper form or online, for: Petitioners filing Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, for non-cap H-1B petitions. Petitioners filing Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, for H-1B cap petitions. Certain F-1 students who already have a pending Form I-765, Application for Employment Authorization, or when filed together with Form I-765, if they are filing under 1 of the following categories: (c)(3)(A) – Pre-Completion OPT; (c)(3)(B) – Post-Completion OPT; and (c)(3)(C) – 24-month Extension of OPT for STEM students. Applicants seeking a change of status to F-1, F-2, M-1, M-2, J-1, or J-2 status, when filed together with Form I-539.
I-907 | Request for Premium Processing Service
Filed either via paper form or online, for:
- Petitioners filing Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, for non-cap H-1B petitions.
- Petitioners filing Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, for H-1B cap petitions.
- Certain F-1 students who already have a pending Form I-765, Application for Employment Authorization, or when filed together with Form I-765, if they are filing under 1 of the following categories: (c)(3)(A) – Pre-Completion OPT; (c)(3)(B) – Post-Completion OPT; and (c)(3)(C) – 24-month Extension of OPT for STEM students.
- (c)(3)(A) – Pre-Completion OPT;
- (c)(3)(B) – Post-Completion OPT; and
- (c)(3)(C) – 24-month Extension of OPT for STEM students.
- Applicants seeking a change of status to F-1, F-2, M-1, M-2, J-1, or J-2 status, when filed together with Form I-539.
N-336 | Request for a Hearing on a Decision in Naturalization Proceedings (Under Section 336 of the INA)
N-400 | Application for Naturalization If you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver, you cannot file Form N-400 online. You must file a paper Form N-400 along with the appropriate waiver request, if you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver.
N-400 | Application for Naturalization
If you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver, you cannot file Form N-400 online. You must file a paper Form N-400 along with the appropriate waiver request, if you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver.
N-565 | Application for Replacement Naturalization/Citizenship Document
N-600 | Application for Certificate of Citizenship
N-600K | Application for Citizenship and Issuance of Certificate Under Section 322

#### Upload Completed PDF Online
This option allows you to upload a completed PDF of your form and related evidence.  Attorneys or representatives using this option must also upload Form G-28, Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative . When completing a PDF online for a benefit requestor, attorneys or representatives must make sure the benefit requestor’s valid signature appears on the PDF before uploading. Below is a list of forms you can file using the PDF upload option.
I-129H2A | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker: H-2A Classification You can only file Form I-129H2A online, using the “PDF upload” option. We will reject any Form I-129H2A petitions filed on paper. If you are filing an H-2A petition by paper, you must continue to use Form I-129.
I-129H2A | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker: H-2A Classification
You can only file Form I-129H2A online, using the “PDF upload” option. We will reject any Form I-129H2A petitions filed on paper. If you are filing an H-2A petition by paper, you must continue to use Form I-129.
I-129 | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker We accept Form I-129 for: E-1 and E-2 petitions (including E2-C) H-1B petitions (including H-1B1, H-1B2, and H-1B3) R-1 petitions TN petitions (all categories)
I-129 | Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
We accept Form I-129 for:
- E-1 and E-2 petitions (including E2-C)
- H-1B petitions (including H-1B1, H-1B2, and H-1B3)
- TN petitions (all categories)
I-130 | Petition for Alien Relative
I-131 | Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records Form I-131 Application Type Selection on Form I-131 Travel authorization documents for Temporary Protected Status (TPS) beneficiaries inside the United States Part 1, Box 4 on Form I-131 Advance parole documents for aliens who are inside the United States and advance permission to travel for CNMI long-term residents Part 1, Boxes: 5.A (pending Form I-485) *Form I-485 Receipt number must begin with IOE; 5.C (pending initial Form I-821); 5.D (Deferred Enforced Departure); 5.E (Approved Form I-821D); and 5.G (Approved Form I-918); on Form I-131 Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States under specific parole programs and processes. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else Part 1, Boxes: 6.B(1), (2), or (3) (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative); and 6.D (Family Reunification Task Force Process); on Form I-131 Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States but are not applying under a specific parole program or process. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else Part 1, Box 7 on Form I-131 Initial request for Arrival/Departure Record for parole in place for aliens who are inside the United States Part 1, Box 8.B (Family Reunification Task Force Process) on Form I-131 Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process: Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021; Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022; The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program; The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative; The Central American Minors Program; Certain participants in the FRTF Process; or Other Program or Process Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021) Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022) Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program) Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative) Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program) Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement) Part 1. Box 10.I on Form I-131 (Other Program or Process) Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for aliens initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place under INA section 212(d)(5)(A) who are: Requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States, but not under a specific program or process; or Requesting a new period of parole and who were initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place, but not under a specific program or process, and someone else is filing the request for them. Part 1. Box 11 You cannot change or amend the selected application type after you file online. If you file Form I-131 online for a category that must be filed on paper (for example, to request an advance parole document) but indicate another category to enable online filing, we may deny your Form I-131 and will not refund your Form I-131 filing fee.
I-131 | Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records
Form I-131 Application Type
Selection on Form I-131
Travel authorization documents for Temporary Protected Status (TPS) beneficiaries inside the United States
Part 1, Box 4 on Form I-131
Advance parole documents for aliens who are inside the United States and advance permission to travel for CNMI long-term residents
Part 1, Boxes: 5.A (pending Form I-485) *Form I-485 Receipt number must begin with IOE; 5.C (pending initial Form I-821); 5.D (Deferred Enforced Departure); 5.E (Approved Form I-821D); and 5.G (Approved Form I-918); on Form I-131
- 5.A (pending Form I-485) *Form I-485 Receipt number must begin with IOE;
- 5.C (pending initial Form I-821);
- 5.D (Deferred Enforced Departure);
- 5.E (Approved Form I-821D); and
- 5.G (Approved Form I-918); on Form I-131
Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States under specific parole programs and processes. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else
Part 1, Boxes: 6.B(1), (2), or (3) (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative); and 6.D (Family Reunification Task Force Process); on Form I-131
- 6.B(1), (2), or (3) (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative); and
- 6.D (Family Reunification Task Force Process); on Form I-131
Initial parole documents for aliens who are currently outside the United States but are not applying under a specific parole program or process. This includes individuals applying for themselves or for someone else
Part 1, Box 7 on Form I-131
Initial request for Arrival/Departure Record for parole in place for aliens who are inside the United States
Part 1, Box 8.B (Family Reunification Task Force Process) on Form I-131
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process: Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021; Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022; The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program; The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative; The Central American Minors Program; Certain participants in the FRTF Process; or Other Program or Process
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for certain aliens who are requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States under an existing program or process:
- Certain Afghans paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021;
- Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022;
- The Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program;
- The Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative;
- The Central American Minors Program;
- Certain participants in the FRTF Process; or
- Other Program or Process
Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021) Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022) Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program) Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative) Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program) Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement) Part 1. Box 10.I on Form I-131 (Other Program or Process)
- Part 1. Box 10.B on Form I-131 (Certain Afghans Paroled into the United States after July 31, 2021)
- Part 1. Box 10.C on Form I-131 (Certain Ukrainians and their Immediate Family Members Paroled into the United States on or after Feb. 11, 2022)
- Part 1. Box 10.D on Form I-131 (Filipino World War II Veterans Parole Program)
- Part 1. Box 10.E (1)(2) or (3) on Form I-131 (Immigrant Military Members and Veterans Initiative)
- Part 1. Box 10.F on Form I-131 (Central American Minors Program)
- Part 1. Box 10.G on Form I-131 (FRTF Process as a Ms. L. Settlement Class member or qualifying additional family member under the Ms. L. Settlement Agreement)
- Part 1. Box 10.I on Form I-131 (Other Program or Process)
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for aliens initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place under INA section 212(d)(5)(A) who are: Requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States, but not under a specific program or process; or Requesting a new period of parole and who were initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place, but not under a specific program or process, and someone else is filing the request for them.
Arrival/Departure Record for re-parole for aliens initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place under INA section 212(d)(5)(A) who are:
- Requesting a new period of parole from inside the United States, but not under a specific program or process; or
- Requesting a new period of parole and who were initially paroled into the United States or granted parole in place, but not under a specific program or process, and someone else is filing the request for them.
You cannot change or amend the selected application type after you file online. If you file Form I-131 online for a category that must be filed on paper (for example, to request an advance parole document) but indicate another category to enable online filing, we may deny your Form I-131 and will not refund your Form I-131 filing fee.
Form I-140, Immigration Petition for Alien Workers You may only file online if you are submitting a standalone Form I-140 without any other form (except Form G-28, if you are filing with an attorney or accredited representative) . If you are submitting Form I-140 together with another form, including Form I-485 or Form I-907, you must file by mail. Form I-907 may still be filed by mail to request premium processing after you file your Form I-140 online.
Form I-140, Immigration Petition for Alien Workers
You may only file online if you are submitting a standalone Form I-140 without any other form (except Form G-28, if you are filing with an attorney or accredited representative) .
If you are submitting Form I-140 together with another form, including Form I-485 or Form I-907, you must file by mail. Form I-907 may still be filed by mail to request premium processing after you file your Form I-140 online.
Form I-485, Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status You can file Form I-485 online only if it is a stand-alone, employment-based application. This means you cannot submit it together with any other forms except: •    Form G-28 (if you are an attorney or accredited representative) •    Form I-693 (Report of Immigration Medical Examination and Vaccination Record) •    Form I-485 Supplement A (Adjustment of Status Under Section 245(i)) You cannot submit Form I-485 online with Supplement J at this time. If you already have a pending Form I-485 application with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you may file Supplement A or Supplement J online as a separate, stand-alone form. All other Form I-485 applications must be filed by mail. If you file Form I-485 online and are required to submit Form I-693 or a partial Form I-693 (such as the Vaccination Record), you must open the sealed envelope from the civil surgeon and upload the completed Form I-693 with your application package. You must keep the original Form I-693 and the envelope from the civil surgeon until we make a final decision on your Form I-485. We may review the original Form I-693 and envelope during an interview or ask you to submit the original Form I-693 and envelope as evidence to support your Form I-485.
Form I-485, Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status
You can file Form I-485 online only if it is a stand-alone, employment-based application. This means you cannot submit it together with any other forms except: •    Form G-28 (if you are an attorney or accredited representative) •    Form I-693 (Report of Immigration Medical Examination and Vaccination Record) •    Form I-485 Supplement A (Adjustment of Status Under Section 245(i))
You cannot submit Form I-485 online with Supplement J at this time. If you already have a pending Form I-485 application with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you may file Supplement A or Supplement J online as a separate, stand-alone form.
All other Form I-485 applications must be filed by mail.
If you file Form I-485 online and are required to submit Form I-693 or a partial Form I-693 (such as the Vaccination Record), you must open the sealed envelope from the civil surgeon and upload the completed Form I-693 with your application package. You must keep the original Form I-693 and the envelope from the civil surgeon until we make a final decision on your Form I-485. We may review the original Form I-693 and envelope during an interview or ask you to submit the original Form I-693 and envelope as evidence to support your Form I-485.
Form I-485 Supplement A (Supplement A to Form I-485, Adjustment of Status Under Section 245(i)) You can submit Form I-485 online with Supplement A at the time of filing. If you already have a Form I-485 application pending with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you may file Supplement A online by itself, without any other forms (except for Form G-28, if you are filing as an attorney or accredited representative).
Form I-485 Supplement A (Supplement A to Form I-485, Adjustment of Status Under Section 245(i))
You can submit Form I-485 online with Supplement A at the time of filing. If you already have a Form I-485 application pending with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you may file Supplement A online by itself, without any other forms (except for Form G-28, if you are filing as an attorney or accredited representative).
I-485 Supplement J, Confirmation of Valid Job Offer or Request for Job Portability Under INA Section 204(j) If you already have a Form I-485 application pending with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you can file Form I-485 Supplement J online if you are submitting only the supplement by itself, without any other forms (except for Form G-28, if you are filing as an attorney or accredited representative). If you are requesting to transfer the underlying basis of a pending employment-based Form I-485, do not file Supplement J online.
I-485 Supplement J, Confirmation of Valid Job Offer or Request for Job Portability Under INA Section 204(j)
If you already have a Form I-485 application pending with USCIS (with a receipt number that begins with IOE), you can file Form I-485 Supplement J online if you are submitting only the supplement by itself, without any other forms (except for Form G-28, if you are filing as an attorney or accredited representative). If you are requesting to transfer the underlying basis of a pending employment-based Form I-485, do not file Supplement J online.
Form I-751, Petition to Remove Conditions on Residence
Form I-765 | Application for Employment Authorization You can file online for these categories: (a)(12) – Temporary Protected Status granted; (c)(8) – Application for employment authorization based on pending Form I-589, Application for Asylum or for Withholding of Removal, and applicants for pending asylum under the ABC Settlement Agreement; (c)(9) - Certain family-based and employment-based applicants pending adjustment of status under Section 245 of the Immigration and Nationality Act; (c)(11) – Paroled for urgent humanitarian or significant public benefit purposes; or (c)(19) – Certain pending TPS applicants whom USCIS has determined are prima facie eligible for TPS and who may then receive an EAD as a “temporary treatment benefit” under 8 C.F.R. 244.10(a). Note: If you are filing Form I-765 for a (c)(9) Pending Adjustment of Status category that is fee exempted , do not submit your application through the PDF filing option at this time. If you file Form I-765 for the (c)(9) category, you must pay a fee or submit a fee waiver request. If you are fee-exempt and proceed with paying the fee and submitting your application through the PDF filing option, we will not issue you a refund. If you are filing under a fee-exempt category, you should mail a paper Form I-765 to the address on the Direct Filing Addresses for Form I-765, Application for Employment Authorization webpage to receive the fee exemption.
Form I-765 | Application for Employment Authorization
You can file online for these categories:
- (a)(12) – Temporary Protected Status granted;
- (c)(8) – Application for employment authorization based on pending Form I-589, Application for Asylum or for Withholding of Removal, and applicants for pending asylum under the ABC Settlement Agreement;
- (c)(9) - Certain family-based and employment-based applicants pending adjustment of status under Section 245 of the Immigration and Nationality Act;
- (c)(11) – Paroled for urgent humanitarian or significant public benefit purposes; or
- (c)(19) – Certain pending TPS applicants whom USCIS has determined are prima facie eligible for TPS and who may then receive an EAD as a “temporary treatment benefit” under 8 C.F.R. 244.10(a).
Note: If you are filing Form I-765 for a (c)(9) Pending Adjustment of Status category that is fee exempted , do not submit your application through the PDF filing option at this time. If you file Form I-765 for the (c)(9) category, you must pay a fee or submit a fee waiver request. If you are fee-exempt and proceed with paying the fee and submitting your application through the PDF filing option, we will not issue you a refund. If you are filing under a fee-exempt category, you should mail a paper Form I-765 to the address on the Direct Filing Addresses for Form I-765, Application for Employment Authorization webpage to receive the fee exemption.
Form I-907 | Request for Premium Processing Service You can file Form I-907 if the underlying form is already pending (with a receipt number that begins with IOE) and was filed either by mail or online: Petitioners with a pending Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker, for: E petitions (all categories); H-1Bpetitions (including H-1B1, H-1B2, and H-1B3); H-2B petitions; H-3 petitions; L-1 petitions (including blanket L-1 petitions); O petitions (all categories); P petitions (all categories); Q petitions (all categories); R-1 petitions; and TN petitions (all categories). Petitioners with a pending Form I-140, Immigrant Petition for Alien Worker Certain F-1 students who already have a pending Form I-765, Application for Employment Authorization, or are filing together with Form I-765 under 1 of the following categories: (c)(3)(A) – Pre-Completion OPT; (c)(3)(B) – Post-Completion OPT; or (c)(3)(C) – 24-month Extension of OPT for STEM students. Applicants with a pending Form I-539, requesting a change of status to F-1, F-2, M-1, M-2, J-1, or J-2 status.

N-400 | Application for Naturalization If you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver, you cannot file Form N-400 online. You must file a paper Form N-400 along with the appropriate waiver request.
N-400 | Application for Naturalization
If you are requesting a reduced fee or filing a fee waiver, you cannot file Form N-400 online. You must file a paper Form N-400 along with the appropriate waiver request.
